page: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton3 processor, utilizing the Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a highly polarized performance profile. The instance excels in memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score and a strong rating in large-block read-only memory bandwidth at 184.3 GB/sec. Conversely, raw CPU compute capabilities are relatively limited, with the Passmark CPU score and CPU encryption tests falling into the weak category. Standard integer, compression, and database operations, such as Redis SET operations at 13.53 million operations per second, consistently register in the average range.

The primary tradeoff of the r7g.2xlarge lies in its high memory throughput versus its lower computational efficiency on legacy x86-optimized workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides high memory density per resource unit, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive cryptographic or vector calculations. This server is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing applications that benefit from high memory bandwidth rather than raw CPU compute power.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 184.3 GB/sec. However, raw compute performance is a notable tradeoff, with Passmark CPU and encryption scores falling into the bottom 25%. Standard operations like Redis SET and static web serving perform in the average tier. Qualitatively, the r7g.2xlarge offers high memory density and bandwidth relative to its resource footprint, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads such as caching layers, in-memory databases, and data processing tasks that do not demand intensive cryptographic or vector processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 8 Graviton3 cores and 64 GB RAM. It features top-tier memory bandwidth but exhibits weaker raw CPU compute performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 Graviton3 cores, 64 GB RAM, and high memory bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and high RAM density for memory-bound data processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 8 dedicated AWS Graviton3 (ARMv8 Neoverse-V1) cores at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, delivering 8.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier Passmark memory score and strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive processing
